rulings.Case Title: VATCHE CABAYAN M.D. VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Relief From Anti-Slapp Discovery Stay Re: 1) Purportedly Privileged Documents & 2) Depositions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 11, 2011, line 1. PLAINTIFFS VATCHE CABAYAN M.D., VATCHE CABAYAN MEDICAL CORPORATION'S Motion For Relief From Anti-Slapp Discovery Stay Re: 1) Purportedly Privileged Documents & 2) Depositions. Plaintiffs' motion granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff has leave to try to depose witness Kealoha. The motion is denied as to the rest. The court will not consider any attorney-client privileged documents in any of plaintiffs' moving or responsive papers. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 11, 2011, line 2. PLAINTIFF ANNA FLIDER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is off calendar per moving party. (Notice filed with court on June 22, 2011). =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: TOM J. BENO VS. JPMORGAN CHASE, N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 11, 2011, line 3. DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NA'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to the 1st cause of action for Fraudulent Concealment is sustained without leave to amend. There are no facts to show that defendant concealed information with intent to defraud plaintiff. Defendant's demurrer to the 2nd cause of action for Constructive Fraud is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts that show that it was defendant's employees who instructed plaintiff to stop making loan payments. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: BONNIE COHEN VS. TINA H. FRANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Strike Parts Of The Pltfs Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 11, 2011, line 4. DEFENDANT THE WASHINGTON BAKER ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Strike Parts Of The Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint. Defendant Washington Baker Association's motion to strike portions of plaintiff's second amended complaint is granted. This settlement agreement does not sufficiently affect the vital concerns of the individual such that plaintiff is entitled to emotional and mental damages for Breach of Contract. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: GIUSEPPE PENZATO et al VS. PEDRO REYES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration; Declarations Of Kenneth Seeger And Adam Salvas In Support
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 11, 2011, line 8. DEFENDANTS ADAM SALVAS, KAREN BRAJE'S Motion To Compel Arbitration is denied. Because this action also involves parties who are not subject to the arbitration clause, and issues arising out of the same facts and transactions here, there is serious possibility that by litigating matters in two different forums would lead to inconsistent results. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 11, 2011, line 8. DEFENDANTS ADAM SALVAS, KAREN BRAJE'S Motion To Stay Proceedings is denied. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: DENTPRO FRANCHISE CORP., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. BRAD EVANS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 11, 2011, line 9. DEFENDANT BRAD EVANS'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Defendant's motion to quash service of summons is denied. Plaintiff has met its burden of demonstrating jurisdiction. The Forum Selection Clause applies because defendant assumed the franchise contract. Second, defendant demonstrates specific jurisdictions. The parties had an ongoing relationship, defendant communicated with plaintiff regarding the franchise and remitted payments to plaintiff. The franchisor approved of the assignment based upon the parties conduct since the assignment. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES S. MADOW, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF HIS VS. POST CONSTRUCTION SERVICES, L.P., A CALIFORNIA et al
